env._comment: Default DIAG_BASE_URL for the web-session direct diag path (scripts/ops/diag_fetch.sh). Points at the live Ampere trader 141.145.193.91 (ict-bot-arm), NOT the terminated x86 micro 158.178.210.252 (retired 2026-06-16). Claude Code applies settings.json env over the inherited process env for the Bash tool, so a session whose cloud-env config still carries the stale micro value resolves the correct host from here. DIAG_READ_TOKEN stays a secret env var (never committed); the direct path also needs Full network egress or it falls back to the issue relay.

permissions._comment: Repo-shared allowlist for the VM-side claude-vm-runner. Scoped to read-only diagnostic commands + the two narrow self-heal restart paths (ict-web-api.service via vm-web-api-recover.yml, ict-trader-live.service via system-actions.yml::restart-bot-service). Per docs/claude/system-actions.md and docs/claude/diag-relay.md, restarting ict-web-api has no trading blast radius (read-only API). Adding entries: mirror the audit pattern in scripts/ops/*.sh — narrow as possible.

hooks._comment: MANDATORY SESSION-START CONTRACT — emitted FIRST so it is the top of every session context, including context-compaction resumes (the failure mode 2026-06-23: a resumed session executed a system-report without reading the canonical rules or the skill, treated 'report mode' as read-only, and shipped findings instead of fixes). Binding clauses: (0) the LIVE COORDINATION BOARD (GitHub issue #6927) is MANDATORY for every session incl. every review sub-session — operator-directed 2026-07-19 to be the FIRST framing any session sees, because parallel sessions still collide even with the merge queue and the merge queue is a committed file (gated on merging) while the board is live/instant; (1) read-first is non-negotiable and a resume does NOT waive it; (2) reviews are WORK sessions that diagnose+fix, not report; (3) definition of done = bugs fixed + backlogs drained + pipeline confirmed end-to-end. Clause 0 stays in sync with docs/claude/coordination-board.md + .claude/skills/session-coordination/SKILL.md (if issue #6927 is ever recreated, update the number in all three). Claude Code on the web injects stdout into the session-init context. Keep in sync with docs/CLAUDE-RULES-CANONICAL.md § Session-start documentation read + § Generation Discipline.
hooks.command: echo '=== SESSION-START CONTRACT — READ BEFORE YOUR FIRST TOOL CALL (non-negotiable) ==='; echo ''; echo '0. YOU ARE PROBABLY NOT THE ONLY CLAUDE WORKING RIGHT NOW — multiple sessions run in parallel and still collide even with the merge queue. Using the LIVE COORDINATION BOARD — GitHub issue #6927 ("Claude Coordination Board") — is MANDATORY for EVERY session, including every review sub-session (/health-review, /performance-review, /ml-review, /system-review). It is NOT the merge queue (docs/claude/session-board.json) and is NOT gated on merging: it is a GitHub issue, so a comment is visible to every live session INSTANTLY (a committed file only propagates through a merge + pull — too late to prevent a collision). (a) At session start, READ the board first: issue_read method=get_comments on #6927, see what every other live session is touching, and ANSWER any open question you can. (b) POST a START comment (session id, branch, and WHICH files / subsystems / PRs you are about to touch) BEFORE your first substantive change — this is the claim that lets other sessions steer clear. (c) POST a QUESTION comment the moment your work might overlap or block another session, and ANSWER questions you can. (d) POST a DONE comment when you wrap so your claim is released. This does NOT replace the merge queue or grant authority (Tier-3 still needs operator approval). Protocol: docs/claude/coordination-board.md + .claude/skills/session-coordination/SKILL.md.'; echo ''; echo '1. READ-FIRST IS MANDATORY. Before ANY tool use, read IN FULL: docs/CLAUDE-RULES-CANONICAL.md (the #1 canonical doc) and the root CLAUDE.md, then the SKILL.md of the skill covering your task. A context-compaction RESUME is a NEW session: pick-up-where-you-left-off does NOT waive this read. If you have not read these this session, your FIRST action is to read them, not to act.'; echo ''; echo '2. THIS IS FULL END-TO-END QA OF THE SYSTEM — a WORK session, NOT a report or scan-and-sweep session. The PURPOSE is to actively HUNT for issues (bugs, correctness gaps, money-at-risk conditions, silent regressions), ROOT-CAUSE them, PROPOSE the exact fix, decide the Tier-2/3 calls WITH the operator, and then ACTUALLY FIX them — together. You FIND the issues so they can be fixed before they become operational catastrophes. Logging a fixable bug to a backlog as a post-it note instead of root-causing and fixing it is a REVIEW FAILURE, not a completed review. This applies to EVERY review session — /health-review, /performance-review, /ml-review, and the /system-review that runs them. Orphaned trades, reconciler-driven closes, undelivered pings, and ANY close that is not TP / SL / strategy-close are BUGS to fix THIS session. Tier-3 calls (strategy / risk / sizing / exit logic) go to the operator WITH the exact proposed change; you keep driving everything else and consult on the decisions only — you can ALWAYS weigh in with the operator (nobody said the whole session must run autonomously), but you must not passively wait for the operator to raise the flags: raising them is your job.'; echo ''; echo '3. DEFINITION OF DONE. Done only when: the bugs are fixed (or the exact Tier-3 fix is in front of the operator for a yes/no), the three review backlogs are drained except items genuinely soaking or awaiting future data, AND the trade pipeline is confirmed working END-TO-END (signal to order to fill to managed exit to correct close reason to graded). A generated report is NOT done.'; echo ''

hooks._comment: Session-init reminder. Two halves: (1) the binding Generation Discipline rules (Rule 1 skill-first lookup + Rule 2 precedents-not-authoritative + Rule 3 compliance-gate-before-merge) from docs/CLAUDE-RULES-CANONICAL.md § Generation Discipline — these govern EVERY output Claude generates and must load before the first user prompt or the precedent-anchoring / non-compliant-merge failure modes recur. (2) the three project-level review skills (split 2026-05-26). Claude Code on the web injects stdout into the session-init context. Keep this echo in sync with § Generation Discipline.
hooks.command: echo '=== GENERATION DISCIPLINE (binding — docs/CLAUDE-RULES-CANONICAL.md § Generation Discipline) ==='; echo ''; echo 'Rule 1 — SKILL-FIRST LOOKUP. Before generating ANY task output (operator instructions, code, workflows, runbooks, PR descriptions, doc edits), your FIRST action is to scan .claude/skills/ for a skill that covers the work. If a skill matches, derive from it. If none matches but one would prevent future inconsistency, PROPOSE ONE IN CHAT (low cost, operator approves). The catalog is the contract.'; echo ''; echo 'Rule 2 — PRECEDENTS ARE NOT AUTHORITATIVE. When referencing any existing artifact (runbook, workflow, code, config) for shape, AUDIT IT against current rules first. Compliant → use it. Non-compliant + touches what you ship → fix in the same PR. Non-compliant + non-blocking → log to docs/claude/health-review-backlog.json. Never silently replicate a non-compliant precedent. Being in the repo is not evidence of being current.'; echo ''; echo 'Rule 3 — COMPLIANCE GATE BEFORE MERGE (binding — § Generation Discipline Rule 3). No work is done and NO PR merges until you audit the finished change against the canonical docs + the skill that governs it. GREEN TESTS ARE NOT COMPLIANCE — a new table on an empty test DB always passes and proves nothing about wiring. DATA WORK: identify the canonical source FIRST and PROJECT over it; a new table/store is the exception (needs operator OK + a backfill), and you MUST verify the feature against LIVE data (a diag pull — confirm existing production records actually appear in the new view) before calling it done. If it does not comply: fix it in the same PR, or ASK the operator before merging if a deviation is genuinely warranted. Never merge known non-compliance silently. Mechanical backstop: the new-table-wiring CI guard fails any PR adding a persistent table without a declared canonical-source relationship.'; echo ''; echo 'Skill catalog: ls .claude/skills/ — read SKILL.md for any skill whose description matches what you are about to do. New skills are cheap; ask before doing work no skill covers.'; ls .claude/skills/ 2>/dev/null | sed 's/^/  /' || echo '  (catalog read failed)'; echo ''; echo '=== PROJECT-LEVEL REVIEW SKILLS: three-way split (2026-05-26) ==='; echo ''; echo '/health-review — TECHNICAL/PIPELINE/DATA health. Reads .claude/skills/health-review/SKILL.md. Output: comms/schema/health_review_response.template.json. Backlog: docs/claude/health-review-backlog.json. Includes weekly compliance-audit rotation against current canonical rules.'; echo ''; echo '/performance-review — TRADING + STRATEGY performance + per-decision A-F grading. Reads .claude/skills/performance-review/SKILL.md. Output: comms/schema/performance_review_response.template.json. Backlog: docs/claude/performance-review-backlog.json. Owns comms/claude_strategy_scores.jsonl.'; echo ''; echo '/ml-review — MODEL + TRAINING lifecycle, promotion/demotion, experiments. Reads .claude/skills/ml-review/SKILL.md. Output: comms/schema/ml_review_response.template.json. Backlog: docs/claude/ml-review-backlog.json.'; echo ''; echo '/system-review — MASTER SYSTEM REVIEW (the work is the REVIEW; the report is just its deliverable). Runs ALL THREE reviews together (individual pings suppressed) AND assesses strategy promotion/demotion readiness, ML training-cycle + soak health, raises flags, finds bugs and PROPOSES/APPLIES the fixes, AND WORKS THE THREE REVIEW BACKLOGS DOWN (docs/claude/{health,performance,ml}-review-backlog.json — drain open items, do not just count them) — it is a WORK session, not a report-generator. A review-coverage guard requires consolidated.review_coverage (strategy_promotion + ml_training_health + soak_status + flags_raised + backlog_drive) or it must STOP. Reads .claude/skills/system-review/SKILL.md. Synthesizes one time-windowed executive report (per-trade dossiers split real/paper/prop, PnL trend, market context, ML fleet), renders comms/reports/<window>/<ts>/report.html, surfaced in both apps via /api/bot/reports. Takes --window=since-last|daily|weekly|monthly. (/system-report is a back-compat alias that runs the same session.)'; echo ''; echo 'For ALL three reviews: Claude pulls the LIVE BOT runtime state itself via the diag relays — NEVER ask the operator to paste a snapshot. Each posts a one-line update to @claude_ict_comms_bot via the send-ping system-action. NONE are code reviews or codebase audits — do NOT fall back to the review or security-review skills, and do NOT ask scoping questions (the scope of each is fixed in its SKILL.md).'

hooks._comment: Session-init CAPABILITY + MULTI-SESSION COORDINATION contract (Workstream-D, 2026-06-28). Surfaces the two gaps the SessionStart contract above did not cover: (1) what tools this session actually HAS (so it stops wasting time on run_workflow / direct VM egress / treating MCP drops as auth expiry), and (2) the cross-session merge protocol that stops two sessions racing a merge into behind-rebase retest churn (hit twice on 2026-06-28). Binding workflow lives in .claude/skills/session-coordination/SKILL.md; the live board is docs/claude/session-board.json. Keep in sync with CLAUDE.md § 'PM-side session capabilities' + docs/CLAUDE-RULES-CANONICAL.md § multi-session coordination.
hooks.command: echo '=== CAPABILITY + COORDINATION CONTRACT (Workstream-D — read before acting) ==='; echo ''; echo 'KNOW YOUR TOOLS (PM-side / Claude Code on the web): run_workflow 403s — drive workflows via LABELLED ISSUES (diag / system-action relays), not workflow_dispatch. Direct VM egress is usually firewalled — live-VM reads go through the vm-diag-snapshot relay (/api/diag/* only) or trainer-vm-diag. The GitHub MCP DROPS intermittently — retry with backoff (2s/4s/8s/16s); NEVER treat the first failure as an expired token or hand off to the operator. No create_label (labels come from bootstrap-labels.yml). Full contract: CLAUDE.md § PM-side session capabilities.'; echo ''; echo 'BIG-SCOPE OR LONG-RUNNING TASK? (many files / multiple subsystems / all three repos / a broad sweep, audit, migration, or refactor) — before diving in head-first, PULL .claude/skills/delegate-work/SKILL.md: decompose into independent units and delegate (parallel in-context tool calls / background Agent fan-out returning structured findings / operator-spawned sub-sessions), single-writer consolidation, then serialize merges via session-coordination. Grinding a big job serially in one context is the failure mode.'; echo ''; echo 'MULTI-SESSION COORDINATION (binding — .claude/skills/session-coordination/SKILL.md). Other sessions may be running. (1) At start, register in docs/claude/session-board.json (active_sessions) and read who else is live + the merge_slot. (2) BEFORE merging ANY PR: list open PRs (real-time truth), CLAIM merge_slot on the board, sync your branch to main LAST (fetch+merge origin/main right before merge so it is not behind), let CI go green on the synced head, merge, then RELEASE the slot. (3) ONE PR = ONE concern — never add unrelated work to a branch with an open PR (it pollutes the PR + invalidates its CI). Start a fresh branch off main for a distinct deliverable.'; echo ''; cat docs/claude/session-board.json 2>/dev/null | sed 's/^/  board| /' || echo '  (session-board.json not found — create it from the schema in the session-coordination skill)'; echo ''

log="$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/logs/notify_hook.log"; mkdir -p "$(dirname "$log")"; cp_line=$(grep -m1 '^## CP-' "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/docs/claude/checkpoints/CHECKPOINT_LOG.md" 2>>"$log" || echo ''); [ -z "$cp_line" ] && { echo "$(date -Iseconds) skip: no CP entry found" >>"$log"; exit 0; }; cp_id=$(echo "$cp_line" | grep -oE 'CP-[0-9-]+' | head -1); title=$(echo "$cp_line" | sed 's/^## CP-[0-9-]* — //'); cd "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR" && { echo "$(date -Iseconds) firing $cp_id" >>"$log"; PYTHONPATH=. python3 scripts/notify_session.py session --checkpoint "$cp_id" --summary "$title" >>"$log" 2>&1; echo "$(date -Iseconds) exit=$?" >>"$log"; } || true
